text.title: Activation Framework: 7 Steps
text.summary: Systematic process from defining the activation event through iterative optimization, with funnel mapping example.

rule.statement: Define activation as the specific behavior that correlates most strongly with D30+ retention — not as completing onboarding or creating an account.
rule.rationale: Internal milestones ("completed setup wizard") are easy to optimize but predict nothing. Retention-correlated behaviors ("shared a doc within 48h", "ran 2 reports within 7d") are the real signal.

example.note: Activation = "completed 5-step onboarding wizard" — optimizing this can increase completion 40% with zero retention improvement.

Activation = "added 3 contacts within 24h" — users who do this have 3x D30 retention vs those who do not. Validate by comparing retention curves for users who did vs did not perform the event within N days of signup.

rule.statement: Calculate baseline activation rate and time-to-activation before making any changes; segment by acquisition channel to identify if the problem is channel-specific.
rule.rationale: Without a baseline, you cannot measure improvement. Channel segmentation often reveals that the problem is concentrated in one source (e.g. paid ads have 15% activation vs organic at 45%).

example.code:
Email tool funnel mapping: Step 1: Signup 100% Step 2: Verify email 70% (-30% — highest drop) Step 3: Connect account 50% (-20%) Step 4: Import contacts 35% (-15%) Step 5: Send first email 25% (-10%) ← ACTIVATION
Priority order: fix Step 1→2 first (biggest absolute drop).

section.title: Steps 5-7: Reduce Friction, Increase Motivation, Iterate

rule.statement: Watch at least 20 session recordings before forming hypotheses; agents cannot replace qualitative pattern recognition from real user behavior.
rule.rationale: Funnel data shows where users drop but not why. Session recordings reveal whether the cause is confusion, missing information, performance issues, or unclear next steps.

list.item: Friction: too many form fields, slow load, required integrations → reduce, pre-fill, defer
Friction: no guidance after signup → add tooltips, coach marks, sample data
Motivation: users can activate but do not want to yet → show value earlier, add social proof
Motivation: wrong user type → segment onboarding by use case
